2604.20331.title: Surrogate modeling for interpreting black-box LLMs in medical predictions
2604.20331.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs), trained on vast datasets, encode extensive real-world knowledge within their parameters, yet their black-box nature obscures the mechanisms and extent of this encoding. Surrogate modeling, which uses simplified models to approximate complex systems, can offer a path toward better interpretability of black-box models. We propose a surrogate modeling framework that quantitatively explains LLM-encoded knowledge. For a specific hypothesis derived from domain knowledge, this framework approximates the latent LLM knowledge space using observable elements (input-output pairs) through extensive prompting across a comprehensive range of simulated scenarios. Through proof-of-concept experiments in medical predictions, we demonstrate our framework's effectiveness in revealing the extent to which LLMs "perceive" each input variable in relation to the output. Particularly, given concerns that LLMs may perpetuate inaccuracies and societal biases embedded in their training data, our experiments using this framework quantitatively revealed both associations that contradict established medical knowledge and the persistence of scientifically refuted racial assumptions within LLM-encoded knowledge. By disclosing these issues, our framework can act as a red-flag indicator to support the safe and reliable application of these models.
2604.20331.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在龐大的數據集上訓練，將廣泛的現實世界知識編碼在其參數中，但其黑箱特性掩蓋了這種編碼的機制和範圍。替代建模使用簡化模型來近似複雜系統，可以提供一條改善黑箱模型可解釋性的途徑。我們提出了一個替代建模框架，定量解釋LLM編碼的知識。對於從領域知識推導出的特定假設，該框架通過在廣泛的模擬場景中進行大量提示，使用可觀察元素（輸入-輸出對）來近似潛在的LLM知識空間。通過在醫療預測中的概念驗證實驗，我們展示了我們框架在揭示LLMs“感知”每個輸入變量與輸出之間的關係的有效性。特別是，考慮到LLMs可能會延續其訓練數據中嵌入的不準確性和社會偏見的擔憂，我們使用這一框架的實驗定量揭示了與既定醫學知識相矛盾的關聯以及在LLM編碼知識中持續存在的科學上被駁斥的種族假設。通過揭示這些問題，我們的框架可以作為紅旗指標，以支持這些模型的安全和可靠應用。
2604.20331.author: Changho Han et.al.
2604.20331.authors: Changho Han, Songsoo Kim, Dong Won Kim, Leo Anthony Celi, Jaewoong Kim, SungA Bae, Dukyong Yoon
